perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, at its sole election, promptly, and in any event within 10 business days, either return or destroy all Confidential Information received by the Recipient and its Representatives (and all copies and reproductions thereof). Any destruction of materials shall be confirmed by the Recipient in writing. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient and its Representatives may each retain copies of the Confidential Information for compliance with applicable laws, rules or regulations, bona fide data retention policies or to establish its rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, at its sole election, promptly, and in any event within 10 business days, either return or destroy all Confidential Information received by the Recipient and its Representatives (and all copies and reproductions thereof). Any destruction of materials shall be confirmed by the Recipient in writing. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient and its Representatives shall each retain copies of the Confidential Information for compliance with applicable laws, rules or regulations, bona fide data retention policies or to establish its rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “may” to “shall”, we create an in-text contradiction within Section 6. Previously, the Recipient had the option of retaining copies of Confidential Information. The alteration forces retention, contradicting the initial instruction to either return or destroy the information upon request. It is now unclear if the Recipient has a choice or is obligated to retain copies regardless of the Disclosing Party's request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return or Destruction of Confidential Information . The Disclosing Party may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient and its Representatives to the Confidential Information. The Recipient shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, at its sole election, promptly, and in any event within 10 business days, either return or destroy all Confidential Information received by the Recipient and its Representatives (and all copies and reproductions thereof). Any destruction of materials shall be confirmed by the Recipient in writing. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient and its Representatives shall each retain copies of the Confidential Information for compliance with applicable laws, rules or regulations, bona fide data retention policies or to establish its rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Recipient 'may' retain copies, giving them an option. The changed text alters this to 'shall' retain copies, making it mandatory. This creates a direct conflict with the Disclosing Party's right to request the return or destruction of all Confidential Information. Now, the Recipient is obligated to retain copies even if the Disclosing Party requests their return, thus a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Recipient is granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees that something in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Recipient is granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'nothing' to 'something' in Section 5 directly contradicts the original intention that the agreement does not transfer ownership of confidential information. This alteration creates an in-text conflict, making it unclear whether ownership is transferred or not, because there is no specification of what is being transfered, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information . The Recipient agrees that something in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Recipient is granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'something in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership', while the contradicted text also states that 'something in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership' but then continues to say 'No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Recipient is granted or implied under this Agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction because the change introduces the idea that ownership might be transferred while the original agreement explicitly denies any transfer of ownership. This contradiction exists in the same place, the contradicted text, which is considered a structural flaw. The contradiction is strong because it fundamentally alters the agreement's stance on ownership, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability, directly conflicting with the original intention.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the foregoing, however, the Disclosing Party shall not assign this Agreement, or delegate its duties or obligations hereunder, without the prior written consent of the Recipient. Any purported assignment or delegation without such consent shall be void and unenforceable.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the foregoing, however, both the Disclosing Party and the Recipient shall not assign this Agreement, or delegate its duties or obligations hereunder, without the prior written consent of the other party. Any purported assignment or delegation without such consent shall be void and unenforceable.
perturbation.explanation: By altering Section 13 to restrict the Recipient from assigning the agreement without consent, we create a contradiction. The original text only limited the Disclosing Party's ability to assign, while the modified text limits both. This creates an in-text contradiction because originally Recipient can assign the agreement without any restrictions but now it is restricted, so it is unclear if the Recipient is now able to assign or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment . This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the foregoing, however, both the Disclosing Party and the Recipient shall not assign this Agreement, or delegate its duties or obligations hereunder, without the prior written consent of the other party. Any purported assignment or delegation without such consent shall be void and unenforceable.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Recipient to assign the agreement without restriction. The changed text imposes a restriction on the Recipient's ability to assign the agreement, requiring the other party's consent. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning that the changed text restricts the Recipient's right to assign, which contradicts the original implicit allowance, which causes the contradiction. Thus, there is an in-text contradiction according to the prompt description.

perturbation.original_text: The parties have executed this Agreement on the last date set forth below. RETAILMENOT, INC By: /s/ Jonathan Kaplan Name: Jonathan Kaplan Title: General Counsel and Secretary Date: 1/10/2017 301 Congress Avenue, Suite 600 Austin, Texas 78701 Attention: General Counsel kaplan@rmn.com VALASSIS COMMUNICATIONS, INC. By: /s/ Edward Taibi Name: Edward Taibi Title: Director Date: 1/10/2017 Address: 35 E. 62 Street NY NY 10065 [Signature Page to Confidentiality Agreement]
perturbation.changed_text: The parties have executed this Agreement on the last date set forth below. RETAILMENOT, INC By: /s/ Jonathan Kaplan Name: Jonathan Kaplan Title: General Counsel and Secretary Date: 1/10/2017 301 Congress Avenue, Suite 600 Austin, Texas 78701 Attention: General Counsel kaplan@rmn.com VALASSIS COMMUNICATIONS, INC. By: /s/ Edward Taibi Name: Edward Taibi Title: Counsel Date: 1/10/2017 Address: 35 E. 62 Street NY NY 10065 [Signature Page to Confidentiality Agreement]
perturbation.explanation: Changing the title of Edward Taibi, from director to counsel. this change introduces a potential in-text contradiction, given that the legal authority and responsibilities associated with a 'Director' are generally distinct from those of 'Counsel'. This shift implies that the individual signing on behalf of Valassis may not possess the requisite corporate authority to bind the company to the agreement, particularly if internal governance policies require director-level authorization for such agreements. This ambiguity could later be exploited to challenge the enforceability of the agreement, arguing that it was not properly executed by an authorized representative of Valassis.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party represents and warrants that it possesses all necessary powers and authority to enter into and be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Edward Taibi's title from 'Director' to 'Counsel'. According to the perturbation explanation, this change introduces a potential in-text contradiction because the legal authority of a Director is generally distinct from that of Counsel. This could imply that Taibi may lack the necessary corporate authority to bind Valassis to the agreement. This directly contradicts the statement in the 'Contradicted Text' that 'Each party represents and warrants that it possesses all necessary powers and authority to enter into and be bound by this Agreement,' thus creating a direct conflict that could affect the enforceability of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions of this Section 20 or any other provision of this Agreement, (i) nothing in this Agreement shall restrict Valassis or any other person from taking the actions set forth in clauses (a) – (g) following termination of the Standstill Period, (ii) nothing in this Agreement shall prevent Valassis or any person acting on its behalf from making any proposal regarding a business combination or other transaction directly to the Board or Chief Executive Officer of RetailMeNot on a confidential basis and from discussing such proposal with such persons if such proposal would not reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make a public announcement and (iii) the Standstill Period shall terminate, and the restrictions set forth in this Section 20 shall terminate and be of no further force and effect, (A) if RetailMeNot enters into a definitive agreement with a party other than Valassis or its Affiliates with respect to, or publicly announces that it plans to enter into, a transaction involving 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then-outstanding equity securities or assets (or equity securities of subsidiaries of RetailMeNot holding assets) constituting 35% or more of the consolidated assets of RetailMeNot and its subsidiaries (whether by merger, consolidation, business combination, tender or exchange offer, recapitalization, restructuring, sale, equity issuance or otherwise) (an “ Alternative Transaction ”), (B) if RetailMeNot publicly announces or confirms that it is in discussions with one or more parties with respect to an Alternative Transaction, or (C) in the event of any announcement or commencement by any person, entity or “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act) of a tender or exchange offer to acquire RetailMeNot’s equity securities which, if successful, would result in such person, entity or group owning, when combined with any other equity securities of RetailMeNot owned by such person, entity or group, 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then outstanding equity securities.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions of this Section 20 or any other provision of this Agreement, (i) nothing in this Agreement shall restrict Valassis or any other person from taking the actions set forth in clauses (a) – (g) following termination of the Standstill Period, (ii) nothing in this Agreement shall prevent Valassis or any person acting on its behalf from making any proposal regarding a business combination or other transaction directly to the Board or Chief Executive Officer of RetailMeNot on a confidential basis and from discussing such proposal with such persons if such proposal would reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make a public announcement and (iii) the Standstill Period shall terminate, and the restrictions set forth in this Section 20 shall terminate and be of no further force and effect, (A) if RetailMeNot enters into a definitive agreement with a party other than Valassis or its Affiliates with respect to, or publicly announces that it plans to enter into, a transaction involving 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then-outstanding equity securities or assets (or equity securities of subsidiaries of RetailMeNot holding assets) constituting 35% or more of the consolidated assets of RetailMeNot and its subsidiaries (whether by merger, consolidation, business combination, tender or exchange offer, recapitalization, restructuring, sale, equity issuance or otherwise) (an “ Alternative Transaction ”), (B) if RetailMeNot publicly announces or confirms that it is in discussions with one or more parties with respect to an Alternative Transaction, or (C) in the event of any announcement or commencement by any person, entity or “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act) of a tender or exchange offer to acquire RetailMeNot’s equity securities which, if successful, would result in such person, entity or group owning, when combined with any other equity securities of RetailMeNot owned by such person, entity or group, 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then outstanding equity securities.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "would not reasonably be expected to require" to "would reasonably be expected to require" in Section 20, the text now implies that Valassis *can* make proposals that *would* require RetailMeNot to make a public announcement, even though the Standstill Period is intended to prevent actions that would force RetailMeNot to disclose information. This is a contradiction because it creates uncertainty about Valassis's ability to propose transactions, because the standstill agreement is suppose to prevent any public disclosure of actions relating to RetailMeNot
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standstill . In consideration of, and only upon, the Confidential Information being furnished to Valassis pursuant to this Agreement, Valassis agrees that, for a period of 12 months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), Valassis shall not, directly or indirectly (through any of its Affiliates or its and their respective Representatives with knowledge of the Possible Transaction), unless specifically approved in advance by the Board of Directors of RetailMeNot (the “ Board ”) in writing: (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal or offer to acquire or effect, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of RetailMeNot, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of RetailMeNot (including any derivative securities, rights or options with economic equivalents of ownership of any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of RetailMeNot or any assets of RetailMeNot, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of RetailMeNot, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) with respect to any voting securities of RetailMeNot, - 5 - (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control, advise, change or influence the management, board of directors, governing instruments, policies or affairs of RetailMeNot, (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action that would reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement (except as required by applicable Law), (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing (except as required by applicable Law), or (g) enter into any agreements, or advise, assist or encourage any other persons (other than its Representatives or RetailMeNot or its representatives) in connection with any of the foregoing. Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions of this Section 20 or any other provision of this Agreement, (i) nothing in this Agreement shall restrict Valassis or any other person from taking the actions set forth in clauses (a) – (g) following termination of the Standstill Period, (ii) nothing in this Agreement shall prevent Valassis or any person acting on its behalf from making any proposal regarding a business combination or other transaction directly to the Board or Chief Executive Officer of RetailMeNot on a confidential basis and from discussing such proposal with such persons if such proposal would reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make a public announcement and (iii) the Standstill Period shall terminate, and the restrictions set forth in this Section 20 shall terminate and be of no further force and effect, (A) if RetailMeNot enters into a definitive agreement with a party other than Valassis or its Affiliates with respect to, or publicly announces that it plans to enter into, a transaction involving 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then-outstanding equity securities or assets (or equity securities of subsidiaries of RetailMeNot holding assets) constituting 35% or more of the consolidated assets of RetailMeNot and its subsidiaries (whether by merger, consolidation, business combination, tender or exchange offer, recapitalization, restructuring, sale, equity issuance or otherwise) (an “ Alternative Transaction ”), (B) if RetailMeNot publicly announces or confirms that it is in discussions with one or more parties with respect to an Alternative Transaction, or (C) in the event of any announcement or commencement by any person, entity or “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act) of a tender or exchange offer to acquire RetailMeNot’s equity securities which, if successful, would result in such person, entity or group owning, when combined with any other equity securities of RetailMeNot owned by such person, entity or group, 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then outstanding equity securities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the condition under which Valassis can make proposals to RetailMeNot. The original text stated that Valassis could make proposals that 'would *not* reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make a public announcement'. The changed text says Valassis can make proposals that 'would reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make a public announcement'. This directly contradicts the 'Standstill' provision in the contradicted text, which aims to prevent Valassis from taking actions that would force RetailMeNot to make public disclosures, creating a strong in-text contradiction. Therefore, the change introduces a clear conflict, making the agreement inconsistent.
